title: Human platelet isolation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"55bv9","text":"Platelets are the primary cellular mediator of thrombosis. This protocol allows for the preparation of washed human platelets. These platelets can then be stimulated or cultured for various purposes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"83f50","text":"Handle platelets gently at all steps to avoid activating them","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"6umi8","text":"Prepare Tyrode's Buffer, or use Tyrode's buffer from a commerical source","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"cuqsv","text":"Human blood is a biosafety hazard. Be familiar and have approval to work under BSL-2 conditions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Use IV butterfly set and draw blood into sodium citrate vaccutainers
Centrifuge at 1200 rpm for 15 minutes at room temperature
Transfer plasma top fraction to a new tube
Dilute plasma with an equal volume of Tyrodes buffer
Add PGI2 to a final concentration of 10uM
Centifuge at 2800 rpm for 5 minutes, decant the supernatant and gently resuspend the platelet pellet into Tyrode's buffer
Dilute the platelets 1:20 in fresh Tyrode's buffer for downstream applications such as flow cytometry, or lyse pellet directly for ELISA or western blotting.
creator: Allen Bennett
